data.title: Palm Oil Slips on Likely Profit-Taking — Market Talk
data.description: Palm oil ended slightly lower after trading in a tight range. Profit-taking ahead of the weekend likely capped positive momentum from firmer soybean oil prices overnight and slowing Malaysian production, Kenanga Futures writes in a note. Chinese buyers remained absent due to the long holiday, which also likely limited any upside, it adds. The Bursa Malaysia Derivatives contract for December delivery fell 4 ringgit to 4,442 ringgit a ton. (kimberley.kao@wsj.com)

data.title: KRKA, TOVARNA ZDRAVIL D.D. - Acquisition of treasury shares
data.description:
POLISH FINANCIAL SUPERVISION AUTHORITY

UNI - EN REPORT No 42 / 2025

Date of issue: 2025-10-03

Short name of the issuer

KRKA

Subject

Acquisition of treasury shares

Official market - legal basis

Unofficial market - legal basis

Contents of the report:

Based on the provisions of the Rules of the Ljubljana Stock Exchange, d. d., Ljubljana and Article

2 of the Commission Delegated Regulation (EU) 2016/1052 Krka, d. d., Novo mesto announces:

With a reference to the public notice from 20 July 2023, Krka is publicly disclosing information on

transactions relating to buy-back programme in the period from 26 September 2025 up to and including

2 October 2025. Krka acquired 10,959 treasury shares in the total value of EUR 2,392,510 on the

Ljubljana Stock Exchange as follows:

- on 26 September 2025: 2,127 shares at a VWAP of EUR 220.27,

- on 29 September 2025: 2,162 shares at a VWAP of EUR 220.00,

- on 30 September 2025: 2,183 shares at a VWAP of EUR 219,10,

- on 1 October 2025: 2,234 shares at a VWAP of EUR 216.13 and

- on 2 October 2025: 2,253 shares at a VWAP of EUR 216.25.

Price is calculated as volume weighted average price (VWAP).

Ilirika d.d., Intercapital d.d. and NLB d.d. were brokers in these transactions. Detailed report on

acquisition of treasury shares attached.

The total number of treasury shares prior to these transactions was 2,326,866 representing 7.096%

of all shares issued.

The total number of treasury shares after these transactions is 2,337,825 representing 7.129% of

all shares issued.

The notice will be published on the Company's website (www.krka.biz) from 3 October 2025 onwards,

for a minimum period of 5 years.

Annexes

File Description

Additional attachments can be found below:

20251003_121751_1183210265_Detailed_report_on_acquisition_of_treasury_shares.pdf

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 1030 GMT on Friday:

** Britain's competition watchdog said it has accepted Spreadex's proposal to sell the business-to-business unit of Sporting Index to appease competition concerns in the UK licensed online sports spread betting market.

** French Prime Minister Sebastien Lecornu ruled out using special constitutional powers to ram the budget through parliament without a vote, putting the onus on lawmakers to agree on a compromise.

** Taiwan's Yageo has succeeded in its $740 million unsolicited bid to buy Japan's Shibaura Electronics as a majority of shareholders tendered to its bid of 7,130 yen ($48.32) per share, it said in a filing.

** Amsterdam-listed private equity firm CVC will acquire a 20% stake in International Schools Partnership (ISP), which owns private schools around the world, it reported.

** Artificial intelligence startups are attracting record sums of venture capital, but some of the world's largest investors warned that early-stage valuations are starting to look frothy, senior investment executives reported.

data.title: Vestas Onshore Orders Jump, Margins Could Disappoint — Market Talk
data.description: Vestas Wind Systems announced a flurry of onshore orders late in the third quarter, but a lack of offshore orders will weigh on the average selling price and could mean margins disappoint, Deutsche Bank analyst John Kim writes. The bank expects the Danish wind-turbine maker to report total third-quarter orders worth 4.9 billion euros, equivalent to 4.8 gigawatts, with revenue of 5.5 billion euros and deliveries of 4.4 gigawatts. It estimates group adjusted EBIT at 301 million euros, with a margin of 5.5%, versus consensus at 6.2%. It updates its forecasts for currencies and trading conditions, lifts its target price to 115 Danish kroner from 118 kroner and keeps its hold rating. Shares fall 3.3% to 123.15 kroner. (dominic.chopping@wsj.com)

data.title: SNG: South Africa's new vehicle sales on track to surpass pre-pandemic levels in 2025
data.description:
First published: 03-Oct-2025 14:19:13

Nqobile Dludla

South Africa's domestic new vehicle sales are on track to exceed pre-pandemic levels for the first time in five years, driven by consumer demand for affordable cars, the president of the auto industry group Naamsa said on Wednesday, 1 October.

A traffic congestion is pictured during the morning peak hour as motorists drive towards the city of Johannesburg, in South Africa, on 16 July 2024. 

Speaking at the organisation’s annual auto conference, Billy Tom told delegates that the industry's performance in the first half of 2025 demonstrated resilience, with strong consumer appetite driving growth despite global and local headwinds.

Domestic new vehicle sales in the first six months of the year increased by 14% compared to the same period last year, with imports rising 30.2%, Tom said. He pointed to "an influx of very affordable models," largely from China.

The South African market for new vehicles "is likely to exceed" 2019 levels for the first time in five years, Tom said. Pre-pandemic sales were at 536,612 units, while new vehicle sales in 2024 were 515,850 units.

The domestic new vehicle market has seen a notable consumer preference shift, with various new entrants to the domestic market, in particular Chinese and more recently Indian car brands, offering options at the more affordable end of the pricing spectrum, said Thato Magasa, Naamsa's vice president for retailing car manufacturers.

"South African consumers used to be very badge-conscious, but with rising costs of living, many consumers have become more interested in value than in the badge," he said.

Since 2024, interest rates in South Africa have been reduced by 125 basis points, while easing inflation and a better economic growth rate have supported the new vehicle market to date, Magasa said.

data.title: Italians take to the streets for Gaza flotilla general strike
data.description:
By Angelo Amante

Tens of thousands of Italians took to the streets across the country on Friday, as part of a day-long general strike called by unions in support of an aid flotilla carrying food to Gaza that was intercepted by Israel this week.

"After what I saw with the flotilla, I thought I couldn't just stand by and do nothing. It's the first time I go to these kind of demonstrations," Mario Mascetti, a protester in Rome, told Reuters.

The CGIL trade union planned demonstrations in more than 100 cities. In the capital, crowds marched from the central Piazza Vittorio towards the main train station, holding union and Palestinian flags, as well as banners.

The strike caused delays and cancellations across Italy's rail network, with more limited disruptions at airports. Metro lines continued operating in both Rome and Milan.

"This is not just any strike. We're here today to defend brotherhood among individuals, among peoples, to put humanity back at the centre, to say no to genocide, to a policy of rearmament," CGIL leader Maurizio Landini said.

Israel has called the aid flotilla a stunt and offered to take aid on the boats into Gaza. It has consistently denied allegations of genocide.

Italy's right-wing government has criticised the strike, with Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni suggesting that people skipping work for Gaza was just an excuse to have a longer weekend break.

Protests in solidarity with the humanitarian convoy that was trying to break Israel's naval blockade have sprung up all over Europe and other parts of the world, but have been particularly widespread in Italy.

Mattia Diletti, a sociologist at Rome's Sapienza University, said the Palestinian cause had always resonated in Italy, both amongst its majority-Catholic population and the left.

"Italy has always been a very political country, characterised by this element," he said.

The national strike watchdog said on Thursday unions had broken rules by not giving enough advance notice for the strike.

Pro-Palestinian protests were due to continue on Saturday, with a mass rally in Rome, capping off several days of demonstrations that have sometimes turned violent, with confrontations with police.

On Thursday night, tens of thousands of people marched peacefully from Rome's Colosseum, while in Turin a conference centre was vandalised and in Milan a statue outside the Duomo cathedral was daubed with red paint and graffiti.

data.title: Banks, metals boost India's equity benchmarks to weekly gains
data.description:
By Bharath Rajeswaran

India's equity benchmarks inched higher on Friday and logged weekly gains, led by banks after the regulator announced a raft of lending reforms and as metals advanced on dollar weakness.

The Nifty 50 rose 0.23% to 24,894.25, while the BSE Sensex added 0.28% to 81,207.17.

The indexes rose about 1% in the week, led by high-weight banks , state-owned lenders and private banks (.NIFPVTBNK) which rose 2.2%, 4.4% and 2.5%, respectively, after the Reserve Bank of India on Wednesday eased rules for lending to capital markets and large companies.

The RBI, which maintained rates as expected, also detailed plans to increase limits on lending against shares and reduce risk weights for non-bank lenders for infrastructure projects.

"The status quo on rates is a relief for banks as a rate cut would have led to contraction of net interest margins, while the relaxation of restrictions on bank loans will aid credit growth," said Prakhar Sharma and Vinayak Agrawal, equity analysts at Jefferies.

Metals rose 1.8% on the day to a record high, lifting weekly gains to 3.9%, supported by a weaker dollar on firming October U.S. rate cut bets and supply disruptions.

The broader small-caps and mid-caps rose 1.8% and 2% this week.

Among individual stocks, Tata Motors rose 6.4%, marking its best week in nearly five months, after setting the effective date for the demerger of its truck and bus unit from the passenger vehicle business.

Sentiment was further lifted as Tata's luxury arm JLR announced a phased resumption of operations following a month-long shutdown.

On the day, V-Mart Retail and Sai Silks surged 16% and 18.4% after strong quarterly business updates.

The drivers for the next leg of the rally will be global liquidity and corporate earnings that kick off next week, said Sonam Srivastava, founder and fund manager at Wright Research PMS.

data.title: Valuno Group clarifies the meaning of the letter of intent regarding joint venture – no impact on existing operations
data.description:
Valuno Group AB (Publ) (“Valuno”) hereby clarifies the meaning of the letter of intent (LOI) communicated yesterday to establish a joint venture together with Direct2Internet AB (publ) (“D2I”) and Citigiro Holding AB (“Citigiro”).

The new company is intended to serve as a regulatory platform, where the parties can leverage D2I’s existing license and regulatory structure while jointly working towards obtaining a future MiCA license. It is important to emphasize that this initiative does not impact Valuno’s existing operations, and that no part of Valuno’s recently acquired banking platform or ongoing business activities will be transferred to the joint venture company. Instead, Valuno should be regarded as a technology provider to the joint venture.

Valuno remains the sole owner of the banking platform acquired from Citigiro, and continues to integrate and further develop the platform within its own corporate structure. The joint venture should instead be seen as a complement to Valuno’s strategy, by providing a strong regulatory foundation that enables both Valuno and its partners to grow in a sustainable and compliant manner.

Valuno’s strategy therefore remains firm: to establish itself as a full-service provider of financial infrastructure, combining traditional payment solutions with blockchain-based innovation. The joint venture adds regulatory strength, without affecting the company’s existing operations, ownership, or business model.

For further information, please contact:

Daniel Sonesson, CEO 

+46 (0)10-2045431

investor@valuno.com  

------------------------------------

Valuno Group Valuno is a Swedish fintech company with a vision of a borderless economy where cryptocurrencies and digital payments are seamlessly integrated into everyday life. The company offers solutions for crypto payments, digital wallets, and related financial services. Valuno has been listed on NGM Nordic SME since July 2019. For more information, visit http://www.investor.valuno.com/?utm_source=chatgpt.comwww.investor.valuno.com.

data.title: One analyst suggests there may finally be a cloud to silver's lining
data.description:
By Jules Rimmer

With a 61% rally so far in 2025, silver has outpaced even gold but technical factors suggest the rise may be peaking.

After its steepling 61% rise this year, silver is riding at a fourteen-year high and within spitting distance of an absolute peak reached 45 years ago.

At $47 per ounce, silver (SI00) is closing in on $49.95 recorded in 1980. In 2025 at least, silver has meant first place and gold (GC00) with a 47% return in second.

Now, though, one commodity analyst believes the squeeze in silver prices is reaching a crescendo and market dislocations have become so acute that they may "self-resolve" in coming weeks.

TD Securities' Daniel Ghalil published an update Thursday in which he observed that "silver lease rates (the interest rate charged to borrow silver) have risen to extreme levels," which demonstrates just how depleted stockpiles are in the inventory of the London Bullion Market Association. The LBMA is the most important wholesale market for gold and silver globally.

Positioning by momentum traders or CTAs has been mounting steadily throughout the summer

Ghalil reported that Indians doubled their imports of silver in September and this buying activity further drained those reserves. The squeeze in silver was then exacerbated by the absence of Chinese players from the market this week, many of whom were celebrating the Golden Week holidays.

The lack of Chinese supply that normally backstops London simply amplified the price movement. Exchanges For Physical, whereby holders of physical silver can deliver that to meet a futures contract, declined sharply, explaining the tightness in the London market. This should ease when Chinese participants return to the market next week, Ghalil predicted. The gap between futures and the physical is wide enough, he argued, that the arbitrage window may remain open for a while, encouraging the sale of physical silver.

This liquidity boost forecast for next week could remove the primary technical driver of the silver price spike that saw it suddenly overtake gold's performance over the summer. Another technical indicator, the RSI (or relative strength index which measures how overbought or oversold a security is) on silver reached 82 this week. In a posting on X Thursday, the Kobeissi Letter ( an investment bulletin) pointed out silver has reached this level five times since 1987 and on each occasion was lower nine months later.

The 'Silver Rush' boom this year, however, is far from a technical phenomenon. As a precious metal, silver is often bracketed with gold as an inflation hedge and a store of value in times of economic uncertainty and geopolitical volatility.

Mohammed El Erian, formerly CEO at Pimco remarked Thursday on X that silver is part of the "debasement trade" (a function of investors concerned about the viability of fiat currencies, especially the dollar DXY ) along with gold and bitcoin (BTCUSD), while also reflecting the notion of U.S. Treasurys as a haven in times of financial stress is no longer applicable.

Silver shares many attributes with gold but is often considered to offer more beta in up markets (a higher beta implies more pronounced moves than the market overall and in silver's instance is partly owing to lower liquidity. This means that inflows and outflows have a disproportionately large impact.). It also boasts industrial applications in solar, electricity generation and energy technology that gold can't compete with generally.

Back in January 1980, when the billionaire Hunt brothers attempted to corner the market in silver and drove the price up to almost $50 per ounce, exchanges took action to prevent the monopoly bid and suspended trading, When the market reopened fully in March of that year, the price crashed to $10 on "Silver Thursday".

Prices may correct next week, according to TD Securities, but probably not to that extent.

-Jules Rimmer

This content was created by MarketWatch, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. MarketWatch is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: NY FED PRESIDENT JOHN WILLIAMS PREPARED REMARKS TO DNB PRESIDENT KLAAS KNOT FAREWELL: CENTRAL BANKS MUST OWN THE RESPONSIBILITY OF PRICE STABILITY
MoneyHero Names Danny Leung Permanent Finance Chief
data.description:
By Colin Kellaher

MoneyHero on Friday said it has named Danny Leung its permanent chief financial officer, removing the interim tag he has carried since last December.

Leung, who joined MoneyHero last year as group director of finance, stepped in as interim finance chief of the Singapore-based operator of online financial comparison platforms after Hao Qian resigned.

Write to Colin Kellaher at colin.kellaher@wsj.com

data.title: MORE NY FED'S WILLIAMS: CENTRAL BANKS MUST HAVE THE INDEPENDENCE OF ACTION AND ABILITY TO ACHIEVE PRICE STABILITY
What $110K gap? Bitcoin futures are ‘aggressively long’ as whales return
data.description:
Key points:

  • Bitcoin futures buy volume indicates that traders are becoming increasingly long-term bullish on BTC this month.

  • The $110,000 “gap” in CME Group’s Bitcoin futures remains unfilled.

  • Bitcoin ETF options experience a spike in popularity as IBIT open interest nears $40 billion.

Bitcoin derivatives traders are flipping “aggressively long” as price squeezes closer to all-time highs.

In a new analysis released on X Friday, J. A. Maartunn, a contributor to the onchain analytics platform CryptoQuant, revealed a significant shift in Bitcoin futures in October.

Bitcoin futures buy volume surges in October

Bitcoin futures markets are undergoing a transformation in sentiment as October gets underway.

As Maartunn showed, net buy volume has surged, and is now outpacing net sell volume by $1.8 billion.

“Futures buyers are stepping up,” he commented alongside a CryptoQuant chart of net taker volumes on largest crypto exchange Binance.

The post was a response to observations by CryptoQuant CEO Ki Young Ju, who noted that Bitcoin’s latest local highs came on the back of sustained buy momentum among derivative-market whales.

“A clear sign of aggressive long positioning,” Maartunn added.

Just days ago, futures markets were hitting the headlines for the opposite reason. 

A weekend “gap” left in CME Group’s Bitcoin futures had become a new short-term BTC price correction target for traders, lying just above $110,000, per data from Cointelegraph Markets Pro and TradingView.

Despite gaps being filled within weeks or days in recent months, sellers failed to initiate a deep enough retracement this week.

As Cointelegraph reported, plans are afoot at CME to make Bitcoin futures trade around the clock, removing the “gap” phenomenon.

Bloomberg analyst: Bitcoin ETFs are “no joke”

The US spot Bitcoin exchange-traded funds (ETFs), meanwhile, took in more than $600 million during Thursday’s Wall Street trading session.

With the week’s total at $2.25 billion at the time of writing, ETF data continued to surprise.

In an X post Friday, James Check, creator of onchain data resource Checkonchain, flagged surging growth in options on the largest spot ETF, BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT).

“The growth of IBIT options is the least discussed, but most significant markets structure shift for Bitcoin since the ETFs themselves,” he argued. 

“Not only did IBIT surpass Deribit, but Options are now larger then futures by open interest.”

Eric Balchunas, a dedicated ETF analyst for Bloomberg, initially reported on IBIT surpassing Coinbase’s Deribit, with the former’s open interest now at $38 billion.

“I told y’all ETFs are no joke.. Fat crypto margins in trouble,” he concluded.

This article does not contain investment advice or recommendations. Every investment and trading move involves risk, and readers should conduct their own research when making a decision.

data.title: The 10-Point: The Wall Street Journal's Guide to the Day's Top News
data.description:
By Emma Tucker

It's Jobs Friday — without a jobs number. The government shutdown means the Bureau of Labor Statistics isn't issuing its monthly jobs report, but alternative statistics from private firms are helping to fill the gap. In Los Angeles, you don't need a report to see that the city's entertainment economy is spiraling downward. Work is evaporating, businesses are closing, and the creative middle class is hanging on by a thread. And young consumers are spending big on blind-box toys, drawn by the thrill of surprise. Our simulation will show you just how addictive it is.

Today's Headlines

President Trump's team is worried that Republicans will take the blame for allowing healthcare subsidies to expire, the issue at the center of the government shutdown.

Delays to a multibillion-dollar deal to send Nvidia's artificial-intelligence chips to the United Arab Emirates are frustrating CEO Jensen Huang and some senior administration officials.

Berkshire Hathaway is coming to the aid of Occidental Petroleum for the second time and getting its hands on a major business in return.

The Trump administration is considering policy to delay the hepatitis B vaccine, the first and only shot most babies in the U.S. receive. Doctors say it can't wait.

Live From The Markets

Markets are dancing around all-time highs. The specific shares you choose to sell can raise or lower how much tax you owe on winners, writes Laura Saunders.

House flipper Opendoor has become a meme stock. But the hype won't make its business any more viable, writes Jonathan Weil.

Read It Here First

Without the government's jobs report, private firms are helping fill the gap.

Payroll processor ADP uses data from more than 26 million workers at its customer firms to gauge employment. Revelio Labs taps figures from LinkedIn and other networking platforms to get readings. There are also consumer and business surveys, and some companies rely on internal data. Bank of America, for example, tracks its customers' card use for insight. Konrad Putzier and Justin Lahart suss out the alternative data sources that economists and investors are looking to in the absence of official numbers.

Pfizer's drug-pricing deal was forged in down-to-the wire negotiations.

All the major pharmaceutical companies had been told they needed to come up with a plan to lower prices and manufacture in the U.S., or face consequences including tariffs. Pfizer wanted to make sure that if the company agreed to a deal, it would secure a reprieve on levies and its manufacturing investments could focus on younger and future products, Liz Essley Whyte, Jonathan D. Rockoff and Peter Loftus report. The final deal came together in the wee hours of the morning before a deadline Trump had set for imposing tariffs.

Expert Take

Q: U.S. farmers expect a bumper crop this year. Why do they need a bailout?

American farmers are harvesting one of the largest crops in history, but they're struggling to find buyers for their soybeans. Trump is considering providing $10 billion or more in aid to U.S. farmers, potentially from tariff revenue. But it's the levies that have the agriculture sector worried, Brian Schwartz, Patrick Thomas and Natalie Andrews report.

A: When Trump placed tariffs on China during his first term, Chinese imports of U.S. soybeans plummeted, devastating farmers' balance sheets. The government sent about $23 billion to farmers to compensate.

While soybean exports recovered in the following years, China has been squeezing American farmers out and has spent heavily to improve the agriculture supply chain in South American countries such as Brazil to fill the gap. From January through August of this year, U.S. soybean exports to China totaled just over 200 million bushels, down from almost one billion bushels during the same period in 2024, according to the American Farm Bureau Federation. Meanwhile, Brazil has shipped more than two billion bushels of soybeans to China during that time.

For U.S. farmers, another year of bumper crops is fueling a glut that is driving down prices for corn and soybeans. Rising costs for equipment, fertilizer and other materials are also crimping profits. U.S. soybean farmers are estimated to lose roughly $100 an acre this year, according to federal data.

See The Story

L.A.'s entertainment economy is looking like a disaster movie.

L.A.'s downward spiral began when the dual strikes by actors and writers ended in 2023. Then entertainment companies cut spending as streaming growth slowed. Work on movies and TV sets isn't steady, but established professionals had rarely gone more than a few months between gigs — until now. Some are scrimping to survive; others have left. Meanwhile, the downturn is rippling through the region's economy.

Happening Today

Sean "Diddy" Combs is set to be sentenced in a New York federal court for two prostitution-related offenses.

Taylor Swift releases her new album, "The Life of a Showgirl." To make it, she reunited with a Swedish producer with a nearly unparalleled track record of hits.

Number Of The Day:

22%

The increase in roasted coffee prices at the grocery store in the past year, more than any other item tracked by the government. Prices at some coffee shops are going up too. People may wince at the price, but they're buying it anyway — and reserving the right to rant.

And Finally...

Buying blind-box toys is all the rage. We unbox the appeal.

Restocks of Labubu blind boxes — which retail for $27.99 each — sell out within minutes. Consumers may want a specific character in the collection of ugly-cute monsters, but the thrill comes from not knowing exactly what they will get. And that keeps young consumers coming back for more. Try your hand at opening a virtual blind box to get a sense of the rush — and the cost.

data.title: Global Equities Roundup: Market Talk
data.description:
The latest Market Talks covering Equities. Published exclusively on Dow Jones Newswires throughout the day.

1014 GMT - EssilorLuxottica looks set to maintain its positive performance, helped by the success of smart glasses, analysts at UBS write in a note. The analysts expect the Ray-Ban maker to report an acceleration in sales for the third quarter, driven by good results in the key North American market, the analysts say. The eyewear group should continue to benefit from new launches and the popularity of smart glasses until its competitors launch a product with comparable features, they say. The stock trades 0.6% higher at 282 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

1000 GMT - Kering's recent management changes and creative shifts seem positive, but a more positive outlook in the luxury sector would give it a boost, Jefferies analysts say in a research note. Earlier this year, the French luxury-goods giant named a new CEO and appointed a new creative chief of Gucci, its flagship label, as it continues to reinvigorate its brands. While these moves seem to boost turnaround hopes, the group could do with some help from an improving industry narrative, Jefferies says. Just like Kering, other luxury players are grappling with a downturn in demand for high-end goods. Jefferies forecasts a decline in third-quarter sales of 9.1% on year on a comparable basis, with Gucci down 15.5%. Shares trade down 0.2% at 286.30 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0950 GMT - Xiaomi's smartphone business is likely to face more competition due to the price advantage of Apple's new release, according to Jefferies analysts. They say in a note that Apple's pricing for the iPhone 17, which turned out to be more aggressive than expected, could pose more headwinds for the Android camp. Jefferies cites early data showing that Android phone sales volumes in China fell by more than 20% in the first week of the iPhone 17's launch. Other major Chinese Android smartphone players that compete with Apple include Huawei and Vivo. The analysts have a buy rating and a target price of HK$66.68 on the stock, which last closed at HK$55.00. (tracy.qu@wsj.com)

0940 GMT - Brunello Cucinelli continues to show the strength of its brand with the fastest organic growth rate in the last seven quarters, Berenberg analysts write in a note to clients. The Italian luxury-fashion firm, known for its cashmere products, posted third-quarter revenue of 335.5 million euros, up around 12% on year. The group noted that its performance in Asia was strong, which could be a positive readacross for its luxury peers despite an expected soft third quarter, the analysts say. Berenberg keeps Brunello Cucinelli's buy rating, and says its a top pick in luxury goods coverage. Shares are up 1.4% at 91.32 euros. (andrea.figueras@wsj.com)

0927 GMT - Banco BPM is the most relevant consolidation option for Italian bank Monte dei Paschi, once it integrates Mediobanca, Equita says in a note. Monte dei Paschi's CEO said that his enlarged group will have a solid capital position and will be able to evaluate further M&A deals. The CEO of Banco BPM, which fended off UniCredit's takeover approach earlier this year, has recognized the possibility of joining forces with Monte dei Paschi. "A possible BMPS-MB-BAMI deal, carried out in a friendly manner, would in our view enjoy broad political support," analyst Andrea Lisi writes. This would create a true national champion with an over 45 billion euro market capitalization, Lisi adds. Monte dei Paschi's shares are flat at 7.61 euros. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0926 GMT - A deal between Banco BPM and Credit Agricole Italia could be worth between 7 billion and 8 billion euros, Keefe, Bruyette & Woods says in a research note. BPM would buy the French bank's Italian retail arm in exchange for BPM shares, stakes in Agos and Anima, according to several press reports. "Such a deal could be win-win for both, but particularly for [BPM], where the [earnings per share] accretion could be double-digit," analysts write. A potential deal should try to keep the resulting Credit Agricole stakes in BPM and Anima below 30% to avoid triggering issues with the Italian government. Since the range of outcomes is wide, it could take months to get clarity on whether a transaction takes place, the analysts say. The market is already pricing an M&A premium into BPM's valuation, they add. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0912 GMT - Schroders is likely to be a key beneficiary from any recovery in demand for active management as clients seek to complement their passive holdings, Citi says in a research note. The U.K. asset manager is expected to deliver among the strongest management fee and pretax-profit growth across Citi's coverage, analysts write. While most traditional asset managers have rerated in recent months, Schroders is the notable exception despite solid flow momentum and strong equities markets. Shares have lagged equity indexes and peers, and even derated, offering a buying opportunity. Citi upgrades its rating on the stock to buy from neutral and lifts its target price to 435 pence from 420 pence. Shares trade 3.1% higher at 391.2 pence in late morning trading. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

0902 GMT - Samsung Electronics' Stargate partnership with OpenAI is expected to drive structural growth in demand for the company's high-bandwidth memory chips, lifting its earnings outlook, CGS analyst Kyunga Lee says in a note. She projects 3Q operating profit to rise 8% from a year earlier to 9.9 trillion won, supported by favorable forex and stronger-than-expected NAND memory prices, and raises its 2026 operating profit forecast by 33% to 54 trillion won. CGS expects the redesigned 1anm HBM3e chip to restore Samsung's technological edge if it meets customers' performance requirements. HBM shipments are forecast to grow 114% in 2026, while the average selling price could fall 3%, Lee adds. CGS maintains its add rating on the stock and raises its target price to KRW125,000 from KRW81,000. Shares last closed at KRW89,000. (jason.chau@wsj.com)

0853 GMT - Shares of European steel companies extend this week's rally, on expectations the European Union will introduce tariffs on steel imports. Shares in Germany's Salzgitter jump 7.7%, taking their gain this week to nearly 24%. Finland's Outokumpu climbs 5%, Thyssenkrupp rises 4.9%, while Acerinox and Aperam both gain more than 2%. The European Union is set to propose 50% tariffs on steel imports above a certain level, in a bid to protect the continent's struggling industry, The Wall Street Journal reported earlier this week. The new levy seems likely to be announced early next week, a move that could benefit companies with EU exposure like Outokumpu and Salzgitter, Deutsche Bank analyst Bastian Synagowitz writes. "This could mark a meaningful inflection point for the sector and support structurally higher margins," Synagowitz says.(aimee.look@wsj.com)

0822 GMT - The Czech koruna falls as Czechs head to the polls Friday and Saturday for the parliamentary elections. Opinion polls suggest the right-wing populist ANO party of billionaire Andrej Babis could win against the ruling center-right coalition government. The market has recently started to price in some risk premium, signaling fiscal expansion compared to the current fiscally conservative government, ING's Chris Turner says in a note. While there is unlikely to be any significant fiscal changes, this could be one of the reasons for the Czech National Bank to maintain tight monetary policy, he says. "Therefore, despite some potential volatility in the short term, we continue to expect the euro to head towards 24.00 koruna." The euro rises 0.1% to 24.265 koruna. (renae.dyer@wsj.com)

0815 GMT - Diploma PLC is one of the safest growth names in the sector, RBC analyst Andrew Brooke writes as he upgrades its rating on the stock and lifts his target price, sending the shares to the top of the FTSE 100 leaderboard. Brooke believes consensus organic revenue growth forecasts can be beaten and sees positive EPS momentum from mergers and acquisitions. RBC raises its rating on the stock to outperform from underperform and target price to 60 pounds from 45 pounds. "We believe the valuation premium is warranted given the outstanding financial metrics and track record," Brooke says. Shares are up 3.1% at 54.20 pounds. (aimee.look@wsj.com)

0759 GMT - J.D. Wetherspoon continues to outperform a challenged U.K. pub market, even though the U.K. consumer backdrop remains fragile, Goodbody analyst Fintan Ryan says in a note. The London-listed pub chain will likely increase its prices during fiscal 2026 to offset rising costs, he adds. The company's fiscal 2025 results beat consensus, he says. Shares are down 3.5% at 6.42 pounds. (cristina.gallardo@wsj.com)

data.title: Stellantis aims to broaden planned South Africa plant's vehicle lineup
data.description:
Key points:
  • Stellantis plans to expand production beyond pickups
  • Market changes prompt automaker to reconsider initial strategy
  • Early production planned for 2027

By Nqobile Dludla

Stellantis intends to expand its product line-up at a planned manufacturing plant in South Africa, moving beyond pick-up trucks as shifting market dynamics prompt a strategy rethink, the company's local manager said.

The automaker announced plans in 2023 to build a greenfield factory in the coastal city of Gqeberha, formerly known as Port Elizabeth, its first plant in South Africa, with the original intention of producing only the Peugeot Landtrek pickup truck.

"The market has changed so dramatically (since then). We believe that just having a pure pickup plant is not as viable," Stellantis South Africa managing director Mike Whitfield told Reuters on Thursday on the sidelines of an auto conference. "We are looking at adding additional products to the plant."

As road infrastructure improves across sub-Saharan Africa and new brands enter the market, consumer preferences are evolving, he said.

"Now, if we look at the new entrants coming from Asia, both parts of Asia - Thailand, India, China - there's a significant change in the competitive landscape of pickups," Whitfield said. "People have access to more affordable vehicles."

He said growth in the market is not actually coming from pickups: "It's coming from smaller vehicles."

By the end of this year Stellantis will be in a position to determine the final product mix. The start of production will be in the second half of 2027, Whitfield said.

The plant, which will be a completely knocked down (CKD) factory - whereby parts are imported or purchased from local suppliers - is part of Stellantis' broader push to grow its footprint in Africa, where it sees long term potential for manufacturing and sales. It will produce cars for the local and African markets.

"We're very much committed to industrialising in South Africa," Whitfield said.

The world's fourth-largest automaker is also planning to launch its joint-venture Chinese Leapmotor C10 range-extended electric SUV in South Africa this year, with more models coming next year.
